title: MetLife (MET) Unveils Solutions to Boost Footprint in Asia
text: MetLife (MET) launches 360Health in Nepal and 360Future in Korea to empower its clients to effectively manage their health conditions and enjoy a happy retirement life.

title: MetLife's Award-winning Health Solution, Retirement Offerings Expand to New Markets in Asia
text: HONG KONG--(BUSINESS WIRE)--MetLife, one of the world's leading financial services companies, is bringing digital healthcare and retirement solutions to new markets across Asia, as it introduces the award-winning 360Health to Nepal and 360Future to Korea. 360Health was created to help users take an active role in managing their health and wellbeing, so they can live healthier for longer. Nepal is the latest market in Asia to benefit from this ground-breaking healthcare solution, which has won 2.

title: MetLife Foundation Reaches New Milestone, Over $1 Billion in Giving
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--MetLife Foundation today announced that it has reached over $1 billion in giving. The Foundation was established in 1976 as an independent charitable arm of Metropolitan Life Insurance Company (MetLife), and since its founding, has made significant contributions to strengthen communities where MetLife has a presence. Today, MetLife Foundation focuses on driving inclusive economic mobility and addressing the needs of underserved and underrepresented communities around.

title: MetLife (MET) Could Be a Great Choice

title: MetLife Sponsors the Upcoming Annual Toyota HBCU New York Classic at MetLife Stadium
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--MetLife, Inc. (NYSE: MET) today announced it is partnering with the Toyota Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCU) New York Classic for the second consecutive year. Featuring Morehouse College and Albany State University, the football game will take place at MetLife Stadium on September 16, 2023, at 3:00 p.m. ET. The event marks the start of HBCU Homecoming week, bringing together fans, students, and alumni from more than 100 HBCUs across the United States.

title: Where Is MetLife Stock Headed?
text: MetLife's stock (NYSE: MET) has lost 13% YTD as compared to the 18% increase in the S&P500 index over the same period.
